editorialSummary.text: Parisian & Viennese-style pastries, sandwiches & coffee served in modern space with an open kitchen.

text.text:
It sounds funny to say, but our first experience with the excellent baked goods of this establishment, was in Honolulu, where they are situated in a coffee shop in Waikiki.
Soz had to try the mother ship!
Busy, but line moved fast.
Nice selection of treats, and great espresso drinks.
Available seating to sit and enjoy ( and bring some home for later, of course!)

text.text:
California Street pitches upward with the kind of steep, unforgiving incline that makes a man regret his past. I wasn't climbing for redemption, though; I was climbing for butter. The target was b. patisserie
. It presents itself as a civilized outpost of Parisian refinement, a place of marble tables and polite conversation. But I was traveling with the little anarchist again, and within minutes, our table had devolved into an active crime scene.
You think you can negotiate with them. You can't. They operate entirely outside the Geneva Conventions of polite dining.
Observe the photographic record. First, consider the glass. It arrived as a pristine, layered strata of cream, dark chocolate, and crumbled cookie rubble. By the time I turned back from my espresso, half the payload was gone. The anarchist had struck with terrifying, surgical precision. You can see his weapon of choice hovering in the frame, aggressively claiming the spoils.
Then, examine the collateral damage. There, abandoned on the white plate next to a breached chocolate cylinder, lies a discarded, smeared spoon. That spoon is me. It is the portrait of a father: hollowed out, deeply sad, and forced to watch a structural masterpiece be ruthlessly dismantled before he could even stake a claim.
And what a masterpiece it was. A dark chocolate fortress, wrapped in a delicate shell and crowned with a treacherous gravel pit of chocolate and white pearls. It was the kind of dessert that requires a permit to construct, utterly defenseless against a high-speed toddler heist.
I looked to the decor for sympathy, but the walls offered none. A bizarre, manic piece of art stared back—a blue-eared creature surrounded by macarons masquerading as flowers and psychedelic mushrooms. It smiled with the unblinking, sugary glee of a witness who finds your suffering completely hilarious.
Five stars. The technique is flawless, the espresso hits like a necessary, bitter truth, and the entire operation serves as a brilliant reminder that in the presence of truly great pastry, all civilization quickly collapses.

text.text:
Visited this place on Saturday
Nice decent place , Tried their famous Amann and croissant. The banana chocolate croissant was amazing. Coffee was great as well .
Very much recommend this place. Try it once.
Removed 1 star for service, did not find the service that great. May be employees were tired and stressed (We visited around 4PM)

text.text:
Came here for the review and it was indeed a decent bakery, but perhaps not quite what it’s hyped up to be.

We got a chocolate croissant, a regular butter croissant, a chocolate kouign amann, and a lemon tart. The croissants are pretty standard — a bit dry, not too buttery (compared to other splendid bakeries in SF like Arsicault). The chocolate croissant doesn’t have a whole lot of chocolate in it. The kouign amann, on the other hand, was really good. It’s flaky and the chocolate is rich. The lemon tart was pretty nice too, but it’s about $8, which I don’t think is worth the price, either.

Overall, it was a nice experience. The staff were nice and helpful, the toilet was clean, and it wasn’t too crowded by the time we got there (around 11:00, no lines at all). But personally, I don’t think the pastry is what it’s cracked up to be.

text.text: Cute patisserie, the coffe is good the pastries wonderful. Rich flavors. Seating is tight. There was a lin to get in. The parking is difficult in the area but that the norm. Service was great and helpful. We would go back when we in town. The sign out front was a little hard to see to find the store. Cute place
